sections.heading: Sector 1: High Speed & Fast Corners
sections.body:
• Turns 1–3 (Opening Complex)

• Brake hard at ~100m into Turn 1; it’s easy to lock up.

• Keep tight through T1, then balance throttle for the left-right sweep of T2 and T3.

• Prioritize clean exits over aggression here — overdriving ruins your run into the next straight.

• Turns 4–8 (Fast Curves + Short Straights)

• These are flowing, high-speed direction changes.

• Light brake or lift for Turn 4, stay committed through 5–6, and balance the car through 7–8.

• Using the curbs here can help, but overusing them leads to track limit warnings or unsettles the car.

sections.heading: Sector 2: Stadium & Underpass Section
sections.body:
• Turns 11–16 (Tight Chicane + Stadium Section)

• This is where lap time is made or lost.

• Brake hard into T11, a tight left-hander, then slow the car right down for the tricky T14–T16 chicane.

• Patience! You’ll want to attack the curbs, but the car can get very unsettled — focus on clean exits, not cutting too much.

• Carrying speed out of T16 is crucial, as it leads onto the longest straight.

sections.heading: Sector 3: Long Straight + Hairpin Finish
sections.body:
• Turns 17–19 (End of Lap)

• After the long straight (with DRS), brake late but controlled into T17 hairpin (second gear).

• It’s a prime overtaking spot — but overshooting kills your run.

• Stay tidy through T18–19 and get back on the throttle early to maximize speed down the main straight.

Car

trackGuide.setupTips:
Setup Tips

• Wings: Medium downforce — you need good top speed but also enough rear stability through the technical middle sector.

• Suspension: Slightly softer to handle curbs, especially under the stadium.

• Differential: More open on throttle helps you get clean traction out of the hairpins.

• Brakes: High brake pressure, but careful balance to avoid front lockups into big stops.

• Tyres: Watch rear temperatures, especially out of slow corners.
trackGuide.drivingTips: Driving Tips • Maximize DRS zones: Use ERS smartly on the two long straights — don’t waste it mid-lap. • Stay smooth in the chicane: The stadium section is bumpy and tight; don’t overcommit, as mistakes here ruin lap flow. • Nail braking points: Turns 1, 11, and 17 are the main overtaking zones; master late but controlled braking. • Watch rear traction: Miami punishes aggressive throttle — be measured on exits. Recommended Miami race strategy in F1 24
